user_message: Can you work with me on whitespace analysis for Blue Yonder Airlines? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-01-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is whitespace analysis.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:09:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:11:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:20:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-O trap (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-O failure mode (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-O -- Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
